summary: Lift the deep-play pass rate of generated games by improving wisdom-pack content, without inflating prompt cost.
_doc: Objective consumed by MetaHarnessCoordinator + templated into the proposer prompt. Pairs with mutable surface wisdom/packs/ (MH.P3) and the PG.6 deep-play gate: the benchmark builds the catalog entries, runs the playability gate INCLUDING the deep-play assertion, and scores the candidate by pass rate. Evidence for the proposer: PlayabilityDebug/*__deep-play.json reports (turn-steal / wedge / NaN traces), QualityIntelligence/incidents.jsonl, and the rubric ledger.

objective.target: Find wisdom-pack edits (new or sharpened guidelines) that raise the deep-play pass rate of freshly generated games vs baseline. Prefer surgical single-hypothesis rules with the concrete failure trace quoted in the rule's why. A candidate is interesting if it (a) lifts deep-play pass rate at modest token cost, OR (b) holds pass rate while consolidating/removing redundant non-critical rules.
objective.proposer_hint: Read PlayabilityDebug deep-play reports and incidents first: every wedges>0 / turnSteals>0 / nanText>0 report is a rule candidate. Generalize the defect CLASS (e.g. 'exactly one turn-advance chokepoint'), never the specific game. Do not delete or downgrade critical rules - the validator blocks it.

frontier_knees.label: cost-optimal
frontier_knees.rule: lowest mean_input_tokens among non-dominated candidates matching baseline deep_play_pass_rate within keep_bar epsilon

frontier_knees.label: balanced
frontier_knees.rule: the non-dominated candidate with the best pass-rate-per-token ratio
